tool_args.label: Metro-North Railroad Financial and Ridership Reports – January 2025

payload.title: Financial and Ridership Reports – January 2025
payload.description: Monthly financial and ridership performance report for MTA Metro-North Railroad for January 2025, including budget vs actual operating results, ridership metrics, staffing levels, and performance indicators.

warnings: source.source_name is accepted as passthrough, but prefer title. Source name label.

source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Total Revenue January 2025 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Total revenue of $69.4 million was $8.7 million lower than the Adopted Budget.

payload.description: Total revenue variance unfavorable by $8.7 million vs Adopted Budget

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total revenue of $69.4 million was $8.7 million lower than the Adopted Budget.

tool_args.label: Total Ridership January 2025 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Through January 2025 ridership was 5.4 million, 7.2% above 2024, 21.8% below 2019 pre-COVID levels

evidence_refs.source_quote: Through January 2025 ridership was 5.4 million, 7.2% above 2024, 21.8% below 2019 pre-COVID levels

tool_args.label: Farebox Revenue January 2025 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Farebox revenue of $48.9 million was $1.0 million higher than the Budget.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Farebox revenue of $48.9 million was $1.0 million higher than the Budget.

tool_args.label: Total Expenses January 2025 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $155.1 million were $15.0 million or 8.8% favorable to the Budget.

payload.scope: Metro-North Railroad, before non-cash liability adjustments

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $155.1 million were $15.0 million or 8.8% favorable to the Budget.

tool_args.label: Commutation Ridership January 2025 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Commutation ridership of 2.2 million was 9.6% above 2024 and 14.5% above the Budget.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Commutation ridership of 2.2 million was 9.6% above 2024 and 14.5% above the Budget.

tool_args.label: Non-Commutation Ridership January 2025 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: Non-commutation ridership of 3.1 million was 5.5% above 2024 and 1.4% below the Budget.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Non-commutation ridership of 3.1 million was 5.5% above 2024 and 1.4% below the Budget.

tool_args.label: Total Headcount January 2025
tool_args.raw_text: At the end of January, the total headcount was 6,709 which was 72 lower than the Budget of 6,781.

evidence_refs.source_quote: At the end of January, the total headcount was 6,709 which was 72 lower than the Budget of 6,781.

tool_args.label: Non-Reimbursable Operating Result YTD Jan 2025
tool_args.raw_text: January YTD non-reimbursable operating results were favorable to the Budget by $9.8 million or 8.1%.

evidence_refs.source_quote: January YTD non-reimbursable operating results were favorable to the Budget by $9.8 million or 8.1%.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio YTD Jan 2025
tool_args.raw_text: The year-to-date January Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 41.8%, which was higher than the Budget.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The year-to-date January Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio was 41.8%, which was higher than the Budget due to higher farebox revenue.

tool_args.label: Adjusted Cost per Passenger YTD Jan 2025
tool_args.raw_text: The year-to-date January Adjusted Cost per Passenger was $24.56, which was lower than the Budget.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The year-to-date January Adjusted Cost per Passenger was $24.56, which was lower than the Budget.

tool_args.label: Revenue per Passenger YTD Jan 2025

evidence_refs.source_quote: The year-to-date January Revenue per Passenger was $9.12, which was higher than the Budget.

tool_args.label: Adjusted indicators for Metro-North/LIRR comparability
tool_args.raw_text: Adjusted Fare Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between Metro-North and the LIRR, and are being presented only at the railroad operating committees.

payload.claim_text: Adjusted Fare Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between Metro-North and the LIRR, and are being presented only at the railroad operating committees. These adjustments are not being used MTA-wide.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad
payload.description: Adjusted indicators include Other Operating Revenue, removal of OPEB retiree expenses, and estimated farebox revenue from Connecticut fare structure equalization

evidence_refs.source_quote: Adjusted Fare Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between Metro-North and the LIRR
